question.text: Under Section 20 of the Depositories Act, what is the maximum fine for contravention of the Act?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per Section 20 of the Depositories Act, a person who contravenes the Act shall be punishable with imprisonment up to 10 years, or with fine up to Rs. 25 crores, or with both. This is without prejudice to any penalty imposed by an adjudicating officer or SEBI. **The Trap:** The Rs. 25 crore fine is the criminal fine under Section 20, separate from the civil penalty of up to Rs. 25 crores under Section 19FA (for unfair business conduct). Both can apply independently. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Section 20 is the ultimate deterrent in the Depositories Act, combining imprisonment and heavy fines, applicable even for attempting contravention or abetment.
